entry.note: For pi agent caching research: installed pi docs show PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long enables extended prompt cache only for direct Anthropic/OpenAI API calls (Anthropic 1h, OpenAI 24h), while pi’s built-in context management remains AGENTS.md/SYSTEM.md loading plus auto-compaction. I found no package-level references to Gemini cachedContent/cached_content, suggesting no native explicit Gemini context-cache wiring in current pi package.

entry.details: Pi built-in provider model catalogs are shipped with each release (docs/providers.md and README). User-level updates should use ~/.pi/agent/models.json for custom providers/models, modelOverrides for built-ins, and /model or pi --list-models to verify. /model reloads models.json when opened, so no restart is needed.

entry.topic: pi custom slash command for subscription model refresh
entry.details: A repo-local extension can register /refresh-models and use ctx.modelRegistry.authStorage + getApiKeyForProvider + registerProvider to refresh OAuth subscription availability without relogin. For each provider, fetch /models (provider-specific candidates), intersect ids with existing built-in provider models, then registerProvider with kept model defs and oauth provider callbacks.

For OpenAI Codex subscriptions, live model discovery requires GET {baseUrl}/codex/models with a semver client_version query (high values like 999.0.0 work), plus Authorization bearer, originator, and chatgpt-account-id headers (account id extracted from JWT claim https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id). GitHub Copilot discovery works via GET {baseUrl}/models with bearer + Copilot headers; response includes policy/capability metadata usable for dynamic model defs.

GitHub Copilot model discovery can return HTTP 400 'missing Editor-Version header for IDE auth' when provider models in registry lack Copilot IDE headers. /refresh-models should merge fallback headers from pi-ai built-in github-copilot model metadata before calling GET {baseUrl}/models, and include response-body snippets in errors for faster diagnosis.

Anthropic subscription tokens can be used to list models via GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models?limit=100 when sent as x-api-key with anthropic-version: 2023-06-01 (and Claude-Code beta headers optional). Using Authorization Bearer alone returns OAuth-not-supported errors on /v1/models.

For /refresh-models, returning addedIds/removedIds from provider refresh and emitting follow-up notify lines (with capped ID list formatting) gives users actionable model-list diffs beyond count-only before/after summaries.

Copilot /models includes internal router IDs and hidden legacy entries. A robust refresh strategy is: parse only picker-enabled entries for newly discovered models, exclude internal accounts/* IDs, but preserve existing known models unless remote policy explicitly marks them disabled. This avoids polluting lists while not dropping known usable models like gpt-4.1/gpt-5.

entry.topic: copilot model list contains non-usable entries
entry.details: GitHub Copilot /models can include embeddings (text-embedding-*), internal router IDs (accounts/*), and synthetic oswe-* entries that are poor fits for Pi model selection. Refresh filtering now excludes those and keeps picker-enabled model entries (even when supported_endpoints is omitted), while still removing models explicitly marked policy.disabled.

OpenAI Codex /codex/models can include hidden internal entries (e.g., codex-auto-review with visibility: hide). Filtering parseOpenAICodexRemoteModels by visibility hide/hidden and supported_in_api=false prevents these from showing up in Pi model selection after refresh.

A repo-local /provider command can safely manage credentials by writing through ctx.modelRegistry.authStorage.set/remove, which persists to ~/.pi/agent/auth.json and updates live session auth state. Include direct and interactive flows plus OAuth guidance to /login.

entry.topic: startup model pruning for noisy providers
entry.details: A lightweight session_start extension can prune noisy model catalogs by re-registering target providers with filtered model lists. Current rule hides date/version-suffixed and preview snapshots (plus internal IDs) for github-copilot/opencode providers, improving /model UX without core TUI changes.

For aggressive /model cleanup requests, implement provider-scoped exact-ID and prefix blocklists inside a session_start model-visibility extension, layered on top of generic date/preview heuristics. This allows targeted hiding for openrouter catalogs while preserving modern keepers.

Model-visibility extension now supports provider-scoped exact-ID and prefix blocklists, allowing iterative user-driven pruning of noisy OpenRouter catalogs (e.g., audio/search/legacy/vendor families) while retaining selected modern IDs.

entry.discovery: Added a Pi extension command at pi/extensions/context.ts for Claude Code-style /context reporting. Pi exposes exact total context usage via ctx.getContextUsage(); component-level breakdown requires approximation from ctx.getSystemPrompt() and current session branch entries.

entry.details: pi/extensions/context.ts /context now emits a normal scrollback custom message (filtered from LLM context via context hook), removes Suggestions/Notes from report output, and aligns session spend values by padding value columns even without detail text.

pi/extensions/context.ts now breaks read_expertise usage out into an Expertise bucket by detecting assistant toolCall blocks with name read_expertise and toolResult messages with toolName read_expertise, excluding those tokens from generic Tool calls/Tool results.

pi/extensions/agent-chain.ts now formats multi-layer read_expertise results as a compact merged snapshot: one shared header, then layer sections in precedence order, with duplicate summaries deduped across all categories before lower-priority layers are printed. Raw fallback keeps the older concatenation path.

read_expertise text should be distilled expertise only: no rebuild/similarity/layer/evidence metadata, no repeated category headings, and generic entries should summarize topic/details/discovery fields as prose instead of raw JSON. Diagnostics remain available in tool details and snapshot JSON.

Default expertise reads should present durable working memory, not changelog history. Keep complete history available through an explicit full/debug mode, and hide task-specific implementation breadcrumbs from the default LLM-facing output.

Concise expertise reads should suppress project-prefixed observations whose project does not match the current repo. Cross-project details belong in full/debug mode unless rewritten as a general principle.

entry.topic: domain-specific strong decisions in concise expertise
entry.details: Concise expertise should filter strong decisions that are clearly tied to a specific domain/tool stack (for example Playwright, SCIM, MPS, Keycloak, or E2E drive mechanics). Keep them available in full/debug mode unless rewritten as a general principle.

entry.summary: Pi auto-discovers every top-level `*.ts` in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/`; helper modules placed there must either move under a non-entry path or export a no-op default factory to avoid `Extension does not export a valid factory function`.

Prompt router now has openai-codex/gpt-5.5-specific effort bias: medium is demoted to low unless classifier confidence >=0.8; high remains available; global max effort cap remains high, and manually-set xhigh is preserved when detected via pi.getThinkingLevel().

entry.details: pi/extensions/*.ts are auto-discovered. probe-thinking-level.ts previously mutated thinking level on session_start by setting minimal then xhigh, leaving sessions at xhigh. prompt-router.ts also had a GPT-5.5 special case that preserved current xhigh instead of lowering it. Fix pattern: keep session_start probes non-mutating, set pi/settings.json defaultThinkingLevel low, and avoid preserving xhigh for openai-codex/gpt-5.5.

entry.role: uv project metadata for prompt-router classifier dependencies; prompt-router invokes classify.py via `uv run --project ~/.dotfiles/pi/prompt-routing ... --classifier t2` to avoid ambient Python missing joblib/sklearn.

entry.task: pi setup refactor and test failure cleanup
entry.outcome: Working tree is clean on main and origin/main at ed9f78d after commits through 4587f30/521ded2/bb5167f; make check-pi-extensions and make lint-python pass.
key_fixes: extension-utils/transcript/tool-reduction now resolve HOME dynamically for tests that mutate process.env.HOME
tool-reduction reduce.py imports optional corpus logging lazily so missing portalocker does not prevent reduction
rules.py tolerates missing jsonschema with minimal id validation for bare-python reducer path
tsc-check.py enables allowImportingTsExtensions and task-registry re-exports TaskState for extension type-check

entry.role: Pi workflow skill for /review-it; final output now requires an Apply options prompt with bug/hardening counts and `/do-it <plan-path>` next-step command.
Pi /plan-it workflow; final presentation now includes copy/paste `/review-it .specs/{slug}/plan.md` and `/do-it .specs/{slug}/plan.md` next-step commands.
Pi /do-it workflow; now asks reports to include copy/paste next-step commands for plan review, execution retry, and created-but-not-executed plan flows.

entry.summary: Implemented optional local lexical focused retrieval for read_expertise in pi/extensions/agent-chain.ts with query/max_results, bounded deterministic ranking/dedup, disposable retrieval-index cache, provider-disabled fallback, and behavioral Vitest coverage in pi/tests/read-expertise-retrieval.test.ts.
entry.validation: Targeted Vitest passed; make test-pytest passed 1166/11 skipped; make lint passed.

entry.decision: Pi /do-it should archive completed plans like Claude Code /do-it
entry.why_good: Keeps workflow parity between Pi and Claude command surfaces and prevents completed specs from remaining in the active .specs/{slug}/ namespace. Updated pi/skills/workflow/do-it.md to set completed date/status and move completed plans to .specs/archive/{slug}/.

entry.decision: Align Pi /review-it and /do-it with Claude workflow parity for review artifacts and deployment gates
entry.why_good: Pi now mirrors Claude's key workflow semantics: /do-it includes a Deployment Procedure gate before archive, and /review-it creates review-{N}/synthesis.md artifacts plus verifies HIGH/CRITICAL findings before accepting them as bugs.
